Outcome Timeframe: Adverse event (group targeted by the intervention)
Study results and measurements: Based on data from 6,373 participants in 1 studies. (Randomized controlled)
Comparator No MTaT: Most common AEs during treatment were fever (0.023/person-day), headache (0.008/person-day), vomiting (0.006/person-day), cough (0.004/ person-day), shivering (0.003/person- day), and nasal congestion (0.002/ person-day).
Intervention Mass testing and treatment: Most common AEs during treatment were fever (0.023/person-day), headache (0.008/person-day), vomiting (0.006/person-day), cough (0.004/ person-day), shivering (0.003/person- day), and nasal congestion (0.002/ person-day).
Certainty of the evidence (Quality of ev: Low Due to serious risk of bias, and serious imprecision 9
Summary: The evidence is very uncertain about the effect of MTaT on adverse events.

Study results and measurements: Based on data from 633 participants in 1 studies. (Observational (non- randomized))
Comparator No MTaT: Three rounds of MTaT were conducted to determine prevalence in the asymptomatic reservoir. MTaT was compared with detection through passive surveillance prevalence. 1st round-moderate to high burden areas -50/28,527 i.e. 0.18% vs 0.06% from passive surveillance; 2nd round-low to high burden areas - 7/11,363 i.e. 0.06% vs 0.03% from passive surveillance; 3rd round- RCD of cryptic cases in 50 households -3/8,467 i.e. 0.03%.
Intervention Mass testing and treatment: Three rounds of MTaT were conducted to determine prevalence in the asymptomatic reservoir. MTaT was compared with detection through passive surveillance prevalence. 1st round-moderate to high burden areas -50/28,527 i.e. 0.18% vs 0.06% from passive surveillance; 2nd round-low to high burden areas - 7/11,363 i.e. 0.06% vs 0.03% from passive surveillance; 3rd round- RCD of cryptic cases in 50 households -3/8,467 i.e. 0.03%.
Certainty of the evidence (Quality of ev: Very low Due to serious risk of bias 10
Summary: The evidence is very uncertain about the effect of MTaT on the prevalence of infection among the group targeted by the intervention.
